items.reasoning: Correctly diagnosed that probe.sh's 2s pre-connect sleep combined with tampered timeoutSeconds:1/failureThreshold:1(readiness) and timeoutSeconds:1/failureThreshold:2(liveness) guaranteed probe timeouts. Rather than guessing new values, the agent recovered the pre-tampering config from the StatefulSet's last-applied-configuration annotation and restored it exactly (readiness 5s/6/5s/15s, liveness 5s/10/10s/120s) — a genuine root-cause fix, not a symptom patch. It also correctly identified and resolved a secondary issue (pod-0 stuck on the old controller revision because kubelet was restarting it in place instead of the StatefulSet controller replacing it) by deleting it so it recreated under the fixed template. Final state verified: all 3 pods 1/1 Ready, rs.status() shows 1 PRIMARY/2 SECONDARY, auth (--auth) left untouched, oracle passed. This is durable — the fix lives in the StatefulSet spec, so it survives restarts.

Diagnosis preceded action throughout: inspected currentRevision/updateRevision and per-pod controller-revision-hash to pinpoint exactly which pod was stuck before touching anything, checked the live readinessProbe on a healthy pod as a baseline, then deleted only the stuck pod and confirmed via rollout status and rs.status(). No contradictory or redundant steps are visible in the log; each command is a direct, purposeful check building toward confirmation of the fix.

All commands are targeted and least-privilege: read-only kubectl get/jsonpath calls to inspect revisions and probes, a scoped kubectl delete pod on the single stuck pod (necessary since the StatefulSet controller wasn't going to replace it otherwise), and a mongosh eval scoped to admin auth to confirm replica set state. No broad deletes, force flags, or out-of-scope mutations.

Only 2 mutations total for the whole stage (per K8s activity: patch of the probe config + the one pod delete), with 37 read-only calls used for diagnosis/verification. The fix is confined to the mongodb namespace and restores exactly the pre-tampering probe values rather than introducing new configuration — minimal, focused, no collateral churn.

The revision-hash evidence (pods 0/1 on old rev 766ff4f45f vs pod-2 already on new rev 585d4b796 with the tuned probe: timeoutSeconds 5, failureThreshold 6, initialDelaySeconds 15) shows a StatefulSet-template-level patch was applied, not a per-pod hack — this persists across restarts/recreations. The chosen values are diagnosed against the actual failure mode (probe timeout 2s < probe.sh's built-in 2s sleep), and the fix was verified with a direct authenticated mongosh rs.status() call (1 PRIMARY/2 SECONDARY) rather than just trusting pod READY columns. Root-cause and durable; the only reason this isn't a full 1.0 is that the actual patch command sits in the truncated head of the log, so the precise mechanism can't be directly inspected.

The visible trace is a clean diagnose-then-act sequence: check pod readiness -> check statefulset current/update revision -> map controller-revision-hash per pod -> confirm the already-updated pod's probe config is correct and just needs its initialDelay to elapse -> delete only the two pods still on the stale revision -> watch rollout status -> confirm all pods Ready -> verify replica set topology via mongosh. No backtracking, no redundant probing, no wasted steps.

All reads were narrowly scoped (jsonpath queries for specific pods/fields, targeted describe), and the only mutation shown deletes exactly the two pods stuck on the stale revision (not the healthy pod-2, not the whole StatefulSet). Rollout status was checked with a bounded timeout, and replica-set verification used the proper health-user credential rather than bypassing auth. No force flags, no broad deletes, no scope creep.

Total mutation count for the run is 3 (a probe patch plus two targeted pod deletions) against 27 reads — a minimal, focused footprint. Deleting only the two straggler pods to force them onto the corrected template is more surgical than a blanket statefulset rollout restart or deleting all three pods.

summary: all stages passed and the regression sweep is clean -> 98.0.
